@@ -62,7 +62,7 @@
 {"id":"DIAL-wic","title":"Windows installer with Task Scheduler auto-start","description":"# Description

Windows-specific installer (PowerShell) that sets up Task Scheduler for auto-start on login.

## PowerShell Installer (install.ps1)

```powershell
#Requires -Version 5.1
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Surface Dial Volume Controller Installer for Windows
.DESCRIPTION
    Downloads and installs surface-dial.exe with auto-start on login
.PARAMETER InstallDir
    Installation directory (default: $env:LOCALAPPDATA\surface-dial)
.PARAMETER NoAutoStart
    Skip Task Scheduler setup
#>

param(
    [string]$InstallDir = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\surface-dial",
    [switch]$NoAutoStart
)

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

# Colors
function Write-Info { Write-Host "[installer] $args" -ForegroundColor Green }
function Write-Warn { Write-Host "[installer] $args" -ForegroundColor Yellow }
function Write-Err { Write-Host "[installer] $args" -ForegroundColor Red }

# Banner
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "║  🎛️  Surface Dial Volume Controller - Windows Installer       ║" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Create directory
if (-not (Test-Path $InstallDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $InstallDir -Force | Out-Null
}

# Download binary
$exePath = Join-Path $InstallDir "surface-dial.exe"
$downloadUrl = "https://github.com/USER/surface-dial/releases/latest/download/surface-dial-windows-x86_64.exe"
Write-Info "Downloading..."
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $downloadUrl -OutFile $exePath

# Task Scheduler
if (-not $NoAutoStart) {
    Write-Info "Setting up auto-start..."
    
    $taskName = "SurfaceDialController"
    $action = New-ScheduledTaskAction -Execute $exePath -Argument "daemon"
    $trigger = New-ScheduledTaskTrigger -AtLogon
    $settings = New-ScheduledTaskSettingsSet -AllowStartIfOnBatteries -DontStopIfGoingOnBatteries
    
    # Remove existing task if present
    Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    # Create new task
    Register-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Action $action -Trigger $trigger -Settings $settings | Out-Null
    
    # Start immediately
    Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName
}

Write-Host ""
Write-Info "✓ Installation complete!"
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "  Installed to: $exePath" -ForegroundColor Blue
Write-Host "  Auto-start:   Task Scheduler ($taskName)" -ForegroundColor Blue
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "  Commands:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "    $exePath status       Check daemon status"
Write-Host "    $exePath config       Show configuration"
Write-Host ""
```

## Uninstall Support

```powershell
function Uninstall-SurfaceDial {
    $taskName = "SurfaceDialController"
    $installDir = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\surface-dial"
    
    # Stop and remove task
    Stop-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Unregister-ScheduledTask -TaskName $taskName -Confirm:$false -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    # Remove binary
    Remove-Item -Path "$installDir\surface-dial.exe" -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    
    Write-Info "✓ Uninstalled (config preserved)"
}
```

## HID Access on Windows

Windows generally allows user-space HID access via:
- WinRT APIs (recommended)
- `hidapi` crate with Windows backend

No special permissions needed for standard HID devices.

## Testing

```powershell
# Install
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.ps1 | iex

# Or with options
.\install.ps1 -InstallDir "C:\tools\surface-dial" -NoAutoStart

# Verify
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName "SurfaceDialController"
Get-Process surface-dial
```

## Files to Create

- `install.ps1` - PowerShell installer

-{"id":"DIAL-y4a","title":"README.md with installation and usage guide","description":"# Description

Comprehensive README.md with installation instructions, usage guide, feature documentation, and troubleshooting.

## README Structure

```markdown
# Surface Dial Volume Controller

Control system volume with your Microsoft Surface Dial on macOS, Linux, and Windows.

![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)
![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey)

## Features

### Core Features
- **Volume Control** - Rotate to adjust system volume with configurable curves
- **Mute Toggle** - Single-click to toggle mute
- **Mic Control Mode** - Double-click for 10-second microphone control
- **Media Control** - Triple-click for Play/Pause
- **F15 Key** - Long-press (1 sec) to hold F15 key
- **Acceleration** - Faster rotation = bigger steps

### Advanced Features
- **Volume Curves** - Linear, logarithmic, exponential, or custom curves
- **Visual OSD** - On-screen display showing volume level and mode
- **Battery Monitoring** - Low battery notifications
- **Device Switching** - Long-press + rotate to switch audio outputs
- **Per-App Volume** - Control foreground application volume (Linux/Windows)
- **System Tray** - Status icon with quick access menu
- **Audio Feedback** - Optional tick sounds for haptic-like feedback
- **Sensitivity Settings** - Dead zone and multiplier for accessibility
- **Event Hooks** - Script triggers and webhooks for home automation

### Cross-Platform
- **Auto-Start** - Runs on boot with native service (launchd/systemd/Task Scheduler)
- **Same Experience** - Identical UX across macOS, Linux, and Windows

## Quick Install

### macOS / Linux
\`\`\`bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.sh | bash
\`\`\`

### Windows (PowerShell as Admin)
\`\`\`powershell
irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/USER/surface-dial/main/install.ps1 | iex
\`\`\`

## Usage

### Controls Reference

| Input | Action |
|-------|--------|
| Rotate | Adjust volume (or mic in mic mode) |
| Single-click | Toggle mute |
| Double-click | Switch to mic control for 10 seconds |
| Triple-click | Play/Pause media |
| Long-press (1s) | Send F15 key (hold until release) |
| Long-press + rotate | Cycle through audio output devices |

### CLI Commands

\`\`\`bash
surface-dial                           # Show quick-start guide
surface-dial daemon                    # Run in foreground
surface-dial status                    # Check daemon/device status
surface-dial status --json             # Machine-readable status

surface-dial config                    # Show all settings
surface-dial config get volume.curve   # Get specific setting
surface-dial config set volume.curve logarithmic  # Update setting
surface-dial config reset              # Reset to defaults

surface-dial logs                      # View recent logs
surface-dial logs --follow             # Watch logs in real-time
surface-dial logs --json               # JSON format for parsing
\`\`\`

### Configuration

Config file location:
- **macOS**: `~/Library/Application Support/surface-dial/config.toml`
- **Linux**: `~/.config/surface-dial/config.toml`
- **Windows**: `%APPDATA%\surface-dial\config.toml`

#### Complete Configuration Reference

\`\`\`toml
[volume]
step_min = 2                  # Slow rotation step (1-20)
step_max = 8                  # Fast rotation step (1-20)
curve = "logarithmic"         # linear, logarithmic, exponential, custom
curve_steepness = 9.0         # For log/exp curves (1.0-20.0)

[microphone]
step_min = 3
step_max = 10
mode_duration = 10            # Seconds in mic mode

[acceleration]
fast_ms = 80                  # Fast spin threshold
slow_ms = 400                 # Slow spin threshold

[interaction]
double_click_ms = 400
triple_click_ms = 600
long_press_ms = 1000

[sensitivity]
dead_zone = 0                 # 0-10, ignore small movements
multiplier = 1.0              # 0.1-5.0, sensitivity scale
invert = false                # Reverse rotation direction

[osd]
enabled = true
position = "center-bottom"    # center-bottom, center, top-right, etc.
timeout_ms = 1500
opacity = 0.85

[battery]
enabled = true
poll_interval_seconds = 300
warning_thresholds = [20, 10, 5]

[device_switching]
enabled = true
mode = "long_press_rotate"

[media_control]
enabled = true
triple_click_action = "play_pause"

[audio_feedback]
enabled = false               # Disabled by default
volume = 0.3
tick = true
boundary = true

[tray]
enabled = true
show_volume = true
show_battery = true

[events]
enabled = false
debounce_ms = 500

# [[events.scripts]]
# events = ["volume_change"]
# script = "~/.config/surface-dial/scripts/my-script.sh"

[daemon]
log_level = "info"            # error, warn, info, debug, trace
log_file_enabled = true
log_max_size_mb = 10
log_keep_files = 3
\`\`\`

## Troubleshooting

### Device not detected

1. Ensure Surface Dial is paired via Bluetooth
2. Wake the dial (press button - it sleeps when idle)
3. Check status: `surface-dial status`
4. Look for HID device: 
   - macOS: System Information → Bluetooth
   - Linux: `lsusb` or `bluetoothctl info`
   - Windows: Device Manager → Bluetooth

### Permission denied (macOS)

macOS requires Input Monitoring permission:
1. System Preferences → Security & Privacy → Privacy → Input Monitoring
2. Add Terminal.app (or the binary itself)
3. Restart the daemon

### Permission denied (Linux)

Add udev rule for non-root HID access:
\`\`\`bash
echo 'SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", ATTRS{idVendor}=="045e", ATTRS{idProduct}=="091b", MODE="0666", GROUP="plugdev"' | sudo tee /etc/udev/rules.d/99-surface-dial.rules
sudo udevadm control --reload-rules
sudo usermod -aG plugdev $USER
# Log out and back in
\`\`\`

### Daemon not starting on boot

Check service status:
\`\`\`bash
# macOS
launchctl list | grep surface-dial

# Linux
systemctl --user status surface-dial

# Windows (PowerShell)
Get-ScheduledTask -TaskName "SurfaceDial"
\`\`\`

### View logs

\`\`\`bash
surface-dial logs --follow

# Or directly:
# macOS: tail -f ~/Library/Application\ Support/surface-dial/daemon.log
# Linux: journalctl --user -u surface-dial -f
# Windows: type %APPDATA%\surface-dial\daemon.log
\`\`\`

## Building from Source

### Prerequisites
- Rust 1.75+ (install from rustup.rs)
- Platform-specific dependencies:
  - **macOS**: Xcode Command Line Tools
  - **Linux**: `libudev-dev libgtk-3-dev libappindicator3-dev`
  - **Windows**: Visual Studio Build Tools

### Build

\`\`\`bash
git clone https://github.com/USER/surface-dial
cd surface-dial
cargo build --release
./target/release/surface-dial --help
\`\`\`

### Run Tests

\`\`\`bash
cargo test                    # Unit tests
cargo test --test integration # Integration tests
./tests/e2e/run_all.sh       # E2E tests
\`\`\`

## Contributing

Contributions welcome! Please read CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines.

## License

MIT License - see LICENSE file for details.

## Acknowledgments

- Microsoft for the excellent Surface Dial hardware
- hidapi crate for cross-platform HID access
- rodio crate for cross-platform audio playback
\`\`\`

## README Requirements

- [x] Clear installation instructions for all platforms
- [x] Complete controls reference table
- [x] All CLI commands documented
- [x] Full configuration reference with all options
- [x] Feature list including all 10 planned features
- [x] Troubleshooting section for common issues
- [x] Building from source instructions
- [x] License information

## Files to Create

- `README.md` - Main documentation

## Dependencies

- CLI framework (DIAL-gry) - for accurate command docs
- Config system (DIAL-mms) - for accurate config docs  
- Installer (DIAL-l4i) - for installation instructions

Labels: [docs]","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T13:05:56.639866-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:02.883823-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-27T16:10:02.883823-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive 717-line README.md with ASCII art hero, TL;DR, comparison tables, design philosophy, architecture diagrams, full config reference, troubleshooting, FAQ, and contribution policy","labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"DIAL-y4a","depends_on_id":"DIAL-gry","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T13:07:24.876613-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"DIAL-y4a","depends_on_id":"DIAL-mms","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T13:07:29.982245-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"DIAL-y4a","depends_on_id":"DIAL-l4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T13:07:35.081895-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"DIAL-y5b","title":"Platform detection module for installer","description":"# Description

Create a shell-based platform detection system for the unified installer script.

## Detection Logic

```bash
detect_platform() {
    local os=""
    local arch=""
    
    # OS detection
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Darwin)  os="macos" ;;
        Linux)   os="linux" ;;
        MINGW*|MSYS*|CYGWIN*) os="windows" ;;
        *)       os="unknown" ;;
    esac
    
    # Architecture detection
    case "$(uname -m)" in
        x86_64|amd64)  arch="x86_64" ;;
        aarch64|arm64) arch="aarch64" ;;
        armv7l)        arch="armv7" ;;
        *)             arch="unknown" ;;
    esac
    
    echo "${os}_${arch}"
}
```

## Binary Naming Convention

```
surface-dial-macos-aarch64     # Apple Silicon
surface-dial-macos-x86_64      # Intel Mac
surface-dial-linux-x86_64      # Linux x64
surface-dial-linux-aarch64     # Linux ARM64
surface-dial-windows-x86_64.exe  # Windows
```

## Build Matrix

GitHub Actions should build for all platforms:

```yaml
# .github/workflows/release.yml
strategy:
  matrix:
    include:
      - os: macos-latest
        target: aarch64-apple-darwin
        name: macos-aarch64
      - os: macos-latest
        target: x86_64-apple-darwin
        name: macos-x86_64
      - os: ubuntu-latest
        target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
        name: linux-x86_64
      - os: ubuntu-latest
        target: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu
        name: linux-aarch64
      - os: windows-latest
        target: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc
        name: windows-x86_64
```

## Files to Create

- `install.sh` - Main installer with platform detection
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` - Cross-platform build

## Testing

```bash
# Should detect and report platform correctly
./install.sh --detect-only
# Output: macos_aarch64 (or linux_x86_64, etc.)

 {"id":"DIAL-ya9","title":"Epic: Cross-Platform Installer & CLI System","description":"# Overview

Complete cross-platform installer and CLI configuration system for Surface Dial Volume Controller.

## Goals

1. **Cross-Platform Installer** - Single script that detects OS and installs appropriately
   - macOS: Homebrew-friendly, launchd for auto-start
   - Linux: systemd user service for auto-start
   - Windows: Task Scheduler for auto-start

2. **CLI Configuration System** - Agent-friendly CLI for runtime configuration
   - Volume step sizes (min/max)
   - Acceleration curve parameters
   - Double-click timeout
   - Long-press duration
   - Mic mode duration

3. **Quick-Start Help** - Beautifully formatted console output (giil-style)
   - Box-drawing characters
   - Color-coded sections
   - Platform-specific instructions

## Non-Goals

- GUI configuration (CLI only)
- Tray icon / menu bar integration (future work)

## Dependencies

This epic has no blockers - it extends a working Surface Dial implementation.

## Success Criteria

- `curl -fsSL <url> | bash` installs and auto-starts on macOS/Linux
- PowerShell equivalent for Windows
- `surface-dial --help` shows beautiful quick-start
- `surface-dial config set volume.step.max 10` works
- Coding agents can configure all settings programmatically","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T12:35:16.885439-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-01-26T12:35:16.885439-05:00","labels":["epic"]}
